topic.name: Educational and Violence Prevention Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving educational standards and addressing violence against women.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 (HEA) to revise and reauthorize HEA programs.
Revises the general definition of an institution of higher education (IHE) to include schools that admit students whose secondary education was conducted in a home school setting.
Document 2: Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994 (VAWA) to add or expand definitions of several terms for purposes of such Act.
Enhancing Judicial and Law Enforcement Tools to Combat Violence Against Women.
Document 3: Revises and reauthorizes programs under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA).
Maintains the requirement conditioning a state's receipt of funds under part A on its developing and implementing academic content and achievement standards.

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Measures
topic.description: This cluster discusses temporary suspensions of duties on various goods under the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on leather footwear for women
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on phosphonic acid
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on diaper shells
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 4: extend the suspension of duty on mixtures of difenoconazole and mefenoxam
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 5: suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures containing fluopyram and tebuconazole
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 6: suspend temporarily the duty on Ethanol
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 7: suspend temporarily the duty on 1,3-propanediaminium
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Election and Representation Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative reforms related to election processes and representation in governance, particularly in the District of Columbia and labor relations.

Document 1: Amends the District of Columbia Home Rule Act to require the Board of Elections and Ethics...
Eliminates the specific alternative of a special election on the same day as the next general election.
Document 2: Treats the District of Columbia as a state for purposes of representation in the House of Representatives and in the Senate.
Increases membership of the House from 435 to 436 Members.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution...
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.
Document 4: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to require that no investigative hearing take place until at least 14 days after the filing of an election petition...
Requires the NLRB to direct an election by secret ballot as soon as practicable but not before 35 calendar days after the filing of the election petition.
Document 5: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the NLRB to decide...whether such unit shall be the employer unit, craft unit, plant unit, or subdivision unit.
Prohibits an election less than 40 calendar days following the filing of an election petition.
Document 6: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the NLRB to require that no investigative hearing take place until at least 14 days after the filing of an election petition...

Requires the Board also, not earlier than seven days after a final determination of the appropriate bargaining unit, to acquire from the employer a list of all employees eligible to vote in the election.
Document 7: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the NLRB to require that no investigative hearing take place until at least 14 days after the filing of an election petition...
Requires the NLRB, in cases where it finds that a question of representation exists, to direct an election by secret ballot as soon as practicable.

topic.name: Temporary Duty Suspensions
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative amendments related to the temporary suspension of duties on various chemical compounds and fibers in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: extends the temporary suspension of duty on ethene, tetrafluoro, oxidized, polymerized reduced, methyl esters, reduced.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: extends the temporary suspension of duty on 2-ethylhexyl (4-chloro-2-methylphenoxy) acetate.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: extends the temporary duty suspension on certain rayon staple fibers.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 4: extends the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers, dyed but not carded, combed, or otherwise processed for spinning.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 5: extends the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fiber, dyed, not carded, combed, or otherwise processed for spinning.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 6: extends the temporary suspension of duty on ethene, tetrafluoro, oxidized, polymerized reduced, methyl esters, reduced.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 7: extends the temporary suspension of duty on mixtures of indoxacarb and inert ingredients.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: FISA and Information Privacy
topic.description: The documents discuss reforms and regulations related to the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA) and the protection of information privacy for journalists and citizens.

Document 1: Prohibits a federal entity from compelling a covered journalist to disclose protected information
Establishes a qualified privilege for journalists to withhold confidential information
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 to place restrictions on the bulk collection of wire or electronic communications
Prohibits a FISA court from authorizing bulk acquisition of wire or electronic communication records
Document 3: Establishes a new process for the FBI to submit applications to a FISA court for business records
Prohibits the FBI from applying for a tangible thing production order unless a specific selection term is used
Document 4: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 to establish a new process for the FBI
Prohibits the FBI from applying for a tangible thing production order unless a specific selection term is used
Document 5: Establishes a new process for the FBI to submit an application to a FISA court for business records
Requires the FBI to include a specific selection term in tangible thing applications
Document 6: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 to establish a new process for the FBI
Prohibits the FBI from applying for a tangible thing production order unless a specific selection term is used
Document 7: Establishes the Financial Stability Oversight Council to identify risks to U.S. financial stability
Promote market discipline by eliminating expectations on the part of shareholders

topic.name: Medicare Policy Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Social Security Act concerning Medicare, focusing on expanding access to care and recognizing additional healthcare providers.

Document 1: Medicare Hospice Care Access Act of 2012
recognize attending physician assistants as attending physicians
Document 2: Protecting Access to Primary Care Act
extend to nurse practitioners, physician assistants, clinical nurse specialists, and certified nurse midwives payment for primary care services
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
exclude certain cancer hospitals from the meaning of subsection (d) hospital
Document 4: Medicare Access to Rural Anesthesiology Act of 2015
provide payment under Medicare part A for anesthesia services
Document 5: Medicare Patient Access to Hospice Act of 2015
recognize attending physician assistants as attending physicians
Document 6: Medicare Clinical Pharmacist Practitioner Services Coverage Act of 2010
provide for coverage of clinical pharmacist practitioner services under Medicare part B
Document 7: Medicare Clinical Pharmacist Practitioner Services Coverage Act of 2008
provide for coverage of clinical pharmacist practitioner services under Medicare part B

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Policy Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative reforms aimed at improving care delivery, medication management, and access to healthcare services under Medicare and Medicaid.

Document 1: establish an integrated chronic care delivery program
promote accountability and better care management for chronically ill patient populations
Document 2: Directs the Comptroller General to review and report to Congress on the retail cost of prescription drugs
Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
Document 3: increase the number of diseases and conditions for which beneficiaries may be targeted for medication therapy management
Requires a Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) sponsor to establish a process
Document 4: require that the primary care services furnished in the two years after enactment of this Act by a physician
extends this 100% of Medicare payment floor
Document 5: require that the primary care services furnished in the two years after enactment of this Act by a physician
extends this 100% of Medicare payment floor
Document 6: increase the number of diseases and conditions for which beneficiaries may be targeted for medication therapy management
Requires a PDP sponsor to offer any willing pharmacy in its network
Document 7: require a group health plan or a health issuer offering group health insurance coverage that provides medical and surgical benefits
Prohibits such a plan or issuer from restricting benefits for any hospital length of stay

topic.name: Regulation of Harmful Substances in Consumer Products
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at regulating harmful substances in food and cosmetics to protect public health.

Document 1: Prohibits the distribution of a food if its container is composed, in whole or in part, of bisphenol A (BPA)
Requires the Secretary to promote and facilitate the use of BPA replacements.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to expand the regulation of cosmetics
Requires the Secretary to establish a list of prohibited or restricted ingredients.
Document 3: Requires manufacturers of cosmetics and ingredients to submit to the Secretary safety data
Establishes three lists for ingredients: a prohibited and restricted list, a safe without limits list, and a priority assessment list.
Document 4: Requires brand owners to submit to the Secretary safety data for the ingredients listed on the cosmetic label
Authorizes the Secretary to order a recall or cease of distribution for a cosmetic that is adulterated, misbranded, or otherwise in violation of the FFDCA.
Document 5: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to publish a plan for the identification of endocrine disrupting substances
To give priority in selecting substances for listing to substances that pose the greatest public health concern.
Document 6: This bill bans food containers composed, in whole or in part, of bisphenol A (BPA)
The FDA must promote and facilitate the use of BPA replacements.
Document 7: This bill bans food containers composed, in whole or in part, of bisphenol A (BPA)
The FDA must review substances that may be found in food, including food additives and food contact substances.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Commodity Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to agricultural commodities, payment structures, and policies affecting farmers and crop production.

Document 1: Repeals direct payments under the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to notify farm owners regarding their opportunity to elect to retain base acres
Document 2: Defines specified terms related to commodity programs
Requires election of either direct payments and counter-cyclical payments or conservation payments
Document 3: Deems the Joint Explanatory Statement submitted by the Committee of Conference for the conference report to accompany H.R. 2419 of the 110th Congress
Defines specified terms related to commodity programs
Document 4: Sets forth provisions concerning the reduction of global warming pollution, energy efficiency, renewable energy
Includes provisions to set a goal of limiting and reducing greenhouse gas emissions
Document 5: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a federal greenhouse gas (GHG) registry
Defines 'GHG emissions' to mean emissions of a GHG
Document 6: Establishes in the Treasury the Clean Energy Investment Fund
Defines 'clean energy technology' to mean a technology related to the production, use, transmission, storage, control, or conservation of energy
Document 7: Sets forth provisions concerning clean energy, energy efficiency, reducing global warming pollution
Establishing a cap-and-trade system for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions

topic.name: Immigration Reform and Family Unification
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative efforts to reform immigration laws in the United States, emphasizing family reunification and the adjustment of status for certain immigrants.

Document 1: Recognizing American Children Act
conditional nonimmigrant status shall include employment and military enlistment authorization.
Document 2: Reuniting Families Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act
increases immigration visas for: (1) unmarried sons and daughters of U.S. citizens; and (2) brothers and sisters of U.S. citizens.
Document 3: Reuniting Families Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act
revises the definition of 'immediate relative' to: (1) mean a child, spouse, or parent of a U.S. citizen or lawful permanent resident.
Document 4: Reuniting Families Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act
establishes the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000.
Document 5: Reuniting Families Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act
provides an 80,640 visa allocation for the unmarried sons and daughters of U.S. citizens.
Document 6: Reuniting Families Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act
the bill also establishes an 80,640 visa allocation for the unmarried sons and daughters of permanent resident.
Document 7: Border Security, Economic Opportunity, and Immigration Modernization Act
establishing a coherent and just system for integrating those who seek to join American society.

topic.name: Federal Program Authorizations
topic.description: This cluster discusses the authorization and funding of various federal programs, including those related to small business innovation, rail safety, and aviation.

Document 1: reauthorize through FY2019 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs
increases the individual small business award levels
Document 2: authorizes appropriations for FY2009-FY2013 for railroad safety
develop a long-term railroad strategy for improving railroad safety
Document 3: authorizes appropriations for FY2008 for the Coast Guard
research, development, test, and evaluation of technologies
Document 4: reauthorizes appropriations to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for FY2010-FY2012
increases passenger facility charge amounts
Document 5: authorizes FY2011 appropriations for the Coast Guard
research, development, test, and evaluation of technologies
Document 6: authorizes appropriations to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for FY2010 and FY2011
establish a program to utilize undergraduate and technical colleges
Document 7: authorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Transportation for FY2012-FY2015
converts to permanent the pilot program for passenger facility charges

topic.name: U.S. Sanctions and Diplomacy in Conflict Regions
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions and promoting diplomatic efforts in conflict-affected regions such as Syria, Iraq, and North Korea.

Document 1: U.S. sanctions, controls, and regulations relating to Syria shall remain in effect until the President certifies that Syria has ceased support for terrorism.

Imposes specified trade, assistance, and military sanctions, as appropriate, on persons or countries that transfer goods or technology so as to contribute to Syria's biological, chemical, nuclear, or advanced conventional weapons programs.
Document 2: Directs the President to implement a regional diplomatic plan in conjunction with the U.N. Security Council to curtail the ongoing civil war in Iraq.
States that it is U.S. policy to undertake a sustained diplomatic effort to engage the governments of regional nations on issues that impact the situation in Iraq.
Document 3: Continues diplomatic, economic, and military sanctions against the government of North Korea as a supporter of international terrorism until the President certifies to Congress that North Korea is no longer engaged in the illegal transfer of missile or nuclear technology.
Expresses the sense of Congress that the Secretary of State should redesignate North Korea as a state sponsor of terrorism.
Document 5: Continues diplomatic, economic, and military sanctions against the government of North Korea as a supporter of international terrorism until the President certifies to Congress that North Korea is no longer engaged in the illegal transfer of missile or nuclear technology.
Directs the President to instruct the U.S. Permanent Representative to the United Nations to use U.S. influence to secure adoption of a Security Council resolution condemning North Korea's action.
Document 6: Expresses the sense of Congress that Congress should vote on a new authorization for use of force resolution that reflects the current Iraq situation, abandons the Bush Doctrine of preemption and unilateralism, and realigns U.S. policy with its long-held priorities of diplomacy, deterrence, and containment.
The United States should intensify diplomatic relations to support the difficult internal steps that the government of Iraq should take to promote national reconciliation.

topic.name: Tax Reform and Relief
topic.description: Documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at reforming tax policies and providing tax relief primarily for middle-class taxpayers.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow in 2008 and 2009: an increase in the basic standard tax deduction
the inclusion in gross income of certain deferred compensation from foreign entities.
Document 2: Extends through 2013 for a taxpayer whose income is $200,000 or less the tax rate reductions and other tax benefits
Increases income tax rates and phases-out personal exemptions and itemized deductions for certain high-income taxpayers.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to replace the marginal income tax rates with a single rate of 17% on individual taxable income
Increases the basic standard deduction and includes an additional standard deduction for dependents.
Document 4: Repeals, after 2006, income tax and estate and gift tax provisions of the Internal Revenue Code
Establishes three income tax brackets at 15, 25, and 30% for individual taxpayers.
Document 6: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to individual income tax rates, the alternative minimum tax
Increases and modifies the standard tax deduction for individual taxpayers.

topic.name: U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Policy
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative measures related to citizenship, immigration, and the rights of individuals in the United States.

Document 1: acknowledgment of God on flag certificates
issued at the request of a Member of the United States House of Representatives or of the United States Senate
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act
limit citizenship at birth to persons born in the United States to mothers who are U.S. citizens or legal residents
Document 3: liquidate or reliquidate certain entries
top-of-the-stove stainless steel cooking ware from the Republic of Korea
Document 4: expands the membership of the Advisory Committee on Minority Veterans
include veterans who are lesbian, gay, bisexual, or transgender
Document 5: prevent any individual convicted of a felony or crime of domestic violence in a foreign court from possessing a firearm in the United States
Document 6: increase criminal penalties for certain removed aliens
illegally reenter the United States
Document 7: provide for the loss of U.S. nationality
by being an unprivileged enemy belligerent

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation aimed at improving public health outcomes through research, prevention, and community outreach initiatives.

Document 1: Directs specified federal agencies participating in the National High-Performance Computing Program to transmit to Congress a cybersecurity strategic research and development plan

Expands permitted National Science Foundation (NSF) grants for basic research on innovative approaches to the structure of computer and network hardware and software that are aimed at enhancing computer security
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for conducting and supporting research on Alzheimer's disease in FY2010-FY2014
Requires the Director of the National Institute on Aging to give priority to conducting and supporting Alzheimer's disease research
Document 3: Authorizes the Director of the Office of Minority Health of the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public health agencies and faith-based organizations to conduct HIV/AIDS prevention, testing, and related outreach activities
Requires the Director of CDC to expand and intensify HIV/AIDS activities in African-American communities
Document 4: Establishes a national forensic science research program to improve, expand, and coordinate federal research in forensic sciences
Directs the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) to identify or coordinate the development of forensic science standards
Document 5: Authorizes the Director of the Office of Minority Health of the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public health agencies and faith-based organizations to conduct HIV/AIDS prevention, testing, and related outreach activities
Requires the Director of CDC to expand and intensify HIV/AIDS activities in African-American communities
Document 6: Authorizes the Director of the Office of Minority Health of the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public health agencies and faith-based organizations to conduct HIV/AIDS prevention, testing, and related outreach activities

Authorizes the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to make grants to faith-based organizations for public health intervention and prevention activities in the African-American community
Document 7: Authorizes the Director of the Office of Minority Health of the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public health agencies and faith-based organizations to conduct HIV/AIDS prevention, testing, and related outreach activities
Requires the Director to expand funding to build the capacity of African-American communities to respond to HIV/AIDS

topic.name: Renewable and Alternative Fuels
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting the use of renewable and alternative fuels through tax credits, infrastructure development, and regulatory changes.

Document 1: require a reduction in the income and excise tax credits for alcohol used for fuel
extend such credits and payments for alcohol fuel mixtures through 2016
Document 2: redefine the term 'cellulosic biomass ethanol'
increases the volume of renewable fuel that gasoline is required to contain for 2008-2012
Document 3: allow an excise tax credit through 2018 for alternative fuels
authorize the Secretary to make grants to manufacturers of light and heavy duty natural gas vehicles
Document 4: requires each manufacturer to ensure that at least 50% of 2011 and 2012 model year automobiles are dual fueled
provides for infrastructure installation subgrants to direct retailers of ethanol fuel blends
Document 5: extend through 2014 the tax credit for producing electricity from renewable resources
authorize appropriations for advanced biofuels research, development, and demonstration
Document 6: allow an excise tax credit through 2019 for alternative fuels and fuel mixtures
requires federal agencies to purchase dedicated alternative fuel vehicles
Document 7: allow an excise tax credit through 2027 for alternative fuels and fuel mixtures
requires 50% of all new vehicles purchased by the U.S. government to be capable of operating on compressed or liquefied natural gas

topic.name: Mortgage Disclosure Regulations
topic.description: Legislative amendments to the Truth in Lending Act aimed at enhancing transparency and consumer protection in mortgage transactions.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information about a consumer credit transaction
Excludes from the computation of such points and fees any compensation paid by a mortgage originator or a creditor.
Document 2: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit a creditor or mortgage broker from arranging a consumer credit transaction secured by the principal dwelling of a consumer without first verifying the consumer's reasonable ability to make the scheduled payments
Prohibits prepayment penalties. Limits finance points, charges, and fees.
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act (TILA) to require the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to exempt from requirements governing escrow or impound accounts affecting certain consumer credit transactions
Includes as a qualified mortgage, with respect to the presumption that a qualified residential mortgage loan meets certain minimum standards.

topic.name: Land Trusts for Native Tribes
topic.description: The documents discuss the authorization and management of land trust acquisitions for various Native American tribes in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to accept title to any additional number of acres of real property located within the boundaries of the original 1857 reservation of the Confederated Tribes of the Grand Ronde Community of Oregon
all real property taken into trust within those boundaries after September 9, 1988, shall be part of the Tribe's reservation.
Document 4: Holds in trust for the Confederated Tribes of Coos, Lower Umpqua, and Siuslaw Indians all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to approximately 14,804 acres of federal land
Makes that land part of the Tribes' reservation.
Document 6: Declares that the United States holds approximately 373 acres of BLM administered land in trust for the Te-moak Tribe of Western Shoshone Indians of Nevada.
Makes such land part of the Tribe's reservation.

topic.name: Middle Grades Education Improvement
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving educational outcomes for middle school students, particularly in high-need areas.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17, to implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans.
Defines 'eligible schools' as those where a high proportion of middle grade students matriculate to secondary schools with graduation rates below 65%.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Education to allot grants to states, based on their share of low-income elementary and secondary school students, to cover part or all of the Advanced Placement (AP) or International Baccalaureate (IB) examination fee low-income students incur.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary to award renewable competitive matching grants to states, local educational agencies (LEAs), or partnerships to increase the number of qualified teachers at high-need schools who are teaching AP or IB courses.
Gives priority in awarding competitive grants to applicants that focus on increasing AP or IB courses in high-need schools.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states to implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans for eligible schools.
Favors LEAs that serve high proportions of poor children and children attending eligible schools.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17, to implement state middle grades needs analyses.
Defines 'eligible schools' as those where a majority of middle grade students are not rated proficient on required state assessments.
Document 6: Permits states to make subgrants to LEAs and partnerships that did not receive a competitive subgrant to assist them in applying for competitive subgrants.
Provides the Secretary with funding to contract for studies that identify promising practices for middle grades education.
Document 7: Gives priority to LEAs that serve high proportions of poor children to implement comprehensive middle school improvement plans.
Defines 'eligible schools' as those where more than 25% of the students exhibit key risk factors for failure.

topic.name: Mortgage and Housing Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming mortgage origination standards, housing finance systems, and enhancing homeownership opportunities.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act (TILA) to prescribe fiduciary standards for originators of residential mortgages
Prohibits steering incentives in connection with mortgage loan origination.
Document 2: Directs the Federal Mortgage Insurance Corporation (FMIC) to dissolve and eliminate Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac
Establishes the FMIC as an independent agency to facilitate a liquid mortgage credit market.
Document 4: Amends the National Housing Act to modify requirements governing the maximum principal loan obligation
Revises eligibility criteria for cash down payment for mortgage insurance.
Document 5: Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to revise requirements for public housing agency inspections
Requires biennial inspections to determine compliance.
Document 6: Amends the National Housing Act to modify requirements for the maximum principal loan obligation
Reduces cash down payment from 3% to 1.5% of the appraised value.
Document 7: Amends the Housing Act of 1949 to require mortgagees to engage in loss mitigation actions as an alternative to foreclosure
Permits the Secretary of Agriculture to authorize the modification of such mortgages.

topic.name: Post Office Designations
topic.description: The documents discuss the official designation of various United States Postal Service facilities in honor of individuals.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 198 Baker Street in Corning, New York, as the 'Specialist Ryan P. Jayne Post Office Building.'
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1335 Jefferson Road in Rochester, New York, as the 'Specialist Theodore Matthew Glende Post Office.'
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 10360 Southwest 186th Street in Miami, Florida, as the 'Larcenia J. Bullard Post Office Building.'
Document 4: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 523 East Railroad Street in Knox, Pennsylvania, as the 'Specialist Ross A. McGinnis Memorial Post Office.'
Document 5: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 220 Elm Avenue in Munising, Michigan, as the 'Elizabeth L. Kinnunen Post Office Building.'
Document 6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 100 Admiral Callaghan Lane in Vallejo, California, as the 'Philmore Graham Post Office Building.'
Document 7: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 442 Miller Valley Road in Prescott, Arizona, as the 'Barry M. Goldwater Post Office.'

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing benefits and support for veterans and their families, particularly related to health care, disability compensation, and family leave.

Document 1: Requires a member of the Armed Forces who is medically retired because of physical disability to be treated as an active-duty member for military health care services and benefits.
Directs the Secretaries of Defense and Veterans Affairs to establish a joint office for the coordination of assistance to members in their transition from military service to civilian life.
Document 2: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act to revise its requirements for exigency leave with respect to employees belonging to the family of members of the Armed Forces.
Entitles coverage by the Act an eligible employee who is a family member caring for a veteran undergoing medical treatment, recuperation, or therapy for a serious injury or illness.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to assign a 30% temporary disability rating upon separation from the Armed Forces for eligible members.
States that disability compensation payments received by a member who receives such temporary disability rating are not subject to offset.
Document 4: Allows the receipt of both military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation with respect to any service-connected disability.

Makes eligible for the full concurrent receipt of both veterans' disability compensation and military retired pay those individuals who were retired or separated from military service due to a service-connected disability.
Document 6: Addresses medical evidence reviews for former members diagnosed with post-traumatic stress disorder or traumatic brain injury.
Any board of review shall review medical evidence with a rebuttable presumption in favor of the former member that PTSD or TBI materially contributed to the discharge.
Document 7: Entitles a surviving spouse to a benefit for the month of a veteran's death if the veteran was receiving veterans' disability compensation or pension.
Includes under the VA's presidential memorial certificate program an individual who dies during active service.

topic.name: Abortion Regulations and Parental Rights
topic.description: The documents discuss federal regulations regarding abortion, particularly focusing on parental involvement and restrictions on transporting minors across state lines for abortion procedures.

Document 1: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
deems such transporting to be a de facto abridgment of the right of a parent
Document 2: imposes criminal penalties on anyone who knowingly or knowingly attempts to perform an abortion based on the sex, gender, color or race of the child
transport a woman into the United States or across a state line for the purpose of obtaining such an abortion
Document 3: perform an abortion knowing that the abortion is sought based on the sex, gender, color or race of the child
transport a woman into the United States or across a state line for the purpose of obtaining such an abortion
Document 4: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
Makes an exception for an abortion necessary to save the life of the minor
Document 5: imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to one year on a physician who performs or induces an abortion on an out-of-state minor in violation of parental notification requirements
Document 6: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
defines 'abortion' to mean the use or prescription of any instrument, medicine, drug, or any other substance
Document 7: makes it an affirmative defense to a prosecution or civil action under this Act that a defendant reasonably believed that the required parental consent or notification took place
imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to one year on a physician who performs or induces an abortion

topic.name: Water and Land Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, conservation, and enhancement of water resources and land use in the Western United States.

Document 1: directs the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to submit a report including a list of projects operated for flood control
carry out at least 15 pilot projects to implement revisions of operational documents
Document 2: establish at least one Forest Reserve Revenue Area within each unit of the National Forest System
provide a dependable source of 25% payments and economic activity for each beneficiary county
Document 3: designates approximately 26,000 acres of land managed by the Bureau of Land Management as the Pine Forest Range Wilderness
requires the Secretary to follow the procedural and substantive requirements of state law in order to obtain and hold any water rights
Document 4: amends the Colorado River Storage Project Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to create and operate a top water bank
construct, operate, and maintain the Navajo Indian Irrigation Project
Document 6: establish a process for declaration of a major disaster for wildfire on federal lands
report to Congress annually on the risk-based factors that influenced management decisions regarding wildfire suppression operations
Document 7: designates specified federal lands in the Monongahela National Forest as wilderness
requires the Secretary to develop a plan to provide for enhanced nonmotorized recreation trail opportunities

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code affecting various tax deductions and credits for individuals.

Document 1: increased limitation on capital losses deduction
amends the Internal Revenue Code
Document 2: deduction from the gross estate for capital losses
amends the Internal Revenue Code
Document 3: permanent tax deduction for qualified tuition
amends the Internal Revenue Code
Document 4: increases deductibility limit for capital losses
provides for inflation adjustment
Document 5: extends exemption from tax penalties for retirement distributions
amends the Internal Revenue Code
Document 6: increases limit on deductibility of capital losses
adjusted for inflation after 2009
Document 7: makes adoption tax credit refundable
amends the Internal Revenue Code
